launcher.release_notes:
Release/cx labs launcher v0.8.0 (#101)

* Document project directories in README

Add a table mapping each top-level directory to its project and a
short description of what it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* cx-labs-launcher: bump to 0.8.0 — DoD framework, Check button, dark mode, AI review

Adds automated scenario verification and observability upgrades across the
launcher, the Go CLI, and the relay Lambda.

Launcher (0.7.2 → 0.8.0)
------------------------
* New "Check" button on every scenario row with a dod.yaml. Opens a modal
  that asks the trainee for a short writeup, runs the CLI's -verify N, and
  renders the report inline with friendly pass/fail messages.
* Dark mode with three-state toggle (Auto / Light / Dark) in the top bar,
  persisted in state.json. Follows macOS system appearance in Auto.
* New verify_scenario Tauri command that locates the CLI (bundled → PATH →
  go-run sibling), sets writeup + OTel env vars, and passes the
  audit-tenant Send-Your-Data key from a signed relay fetch.
* Coralogix audit-tenant OTel routing: Anthropic API key + audit-tenant
  key/domain are both fetched from the relay Lambda on demand and cached
  in-memory (1h TTL). Trainee's own CX_DATA_KEY is no longer used for
  AI-review traces.
* Enriched scenario.verify audit event: parses the CLI report for check
  outcomes and AI verdict and includes them in the event subject.

Go CLI (internal/dod)
---------------------
* New AI review path: rubric-based grading via Anthropic Messages API,
  reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, structured JSON verdict. Advisory only — does
  not gate Complete.
* New OTel instrumentation: chat anthropic span with gen_ai.* attributes,
  flat prompt/completion attributes AI Center indexes on, and per-user
  identity (user.id/email, gen_ai.user.id). OTLP HTTP exporter configured
  via standard OTEL_* env vars.
* New kubectl check type in dod.yaml — runs kubectl get -o jsonpath and
  wraps stdout as {value:...} for JSONPath assertions. Complements the
  existing HTTP-request check type.
* pass_message / fail_message overrides on Check for trainee-facing prose
  in the report instead of the raw assertion diagnostic.
* Time-window sentinels in DataPrime queries: metadata.startDate: "now-15m"
  gets resolved to RFC3339 timestamps per poll attempt (sliding window,
  prevents stale logs from satisfying checks).
* Reference answer in dod.yaml — shown ONLY when checks pass AND AI
  review's rubric fails (corrective nudge, not a spoiler). Retry hint
  shown otherwise.
* Trainee writeup now echoed in the report under the AI review section.

Scenarios
---------
* Scenario 001 (Console Logs): redeployed with a LOG_LEVEL misconfig
  (CRITICAL) so the fix genuinely matches the "why no logs" question.
  Added dod.yaml with two required checks (logs arriving + LOG_LEVEL
  equals INFO/DEBUG via kubectl), AI review, reference answer.
* Scenario 002 (TCO debug logs): app now creates a "block" TCO policy
  named scenario-002-debug-tco instead of "medium". Added parsing rule
  group that maps text.level → severity so debug logs classify correctly.
  Added SIGTERM cleanup that removes both TCO policy and parsing rule
  group when the scenario is uninstalled. Log body slimmed down to
  {timestamp, level, text, message} — applicationName/subsystemName come
  from OTel resource attrs, not the body. Added dod.yaml with TCO API
  check + AI review.
* Scenario 003 (Logs parsing workflow): reordered rule group so
  "Block DEBUG to archive" is at position 1 (short-circuits everything)
  and Parse body / JSON extract / Extract type follow. dod.yaml now
  checks both the reorder AND that DEBUG-severity logs actually arrive in
  Coralogix (positive end-to-end signal). Added SIGTERM cleanup.

Relay Lambda
------------
* New POST /keys/anthropic — signed fetch of the shared Anthropic API key
  from AWS Secrets Manager.
* New POST /keys/coralogix-audit — signed fetch of the audit-tenant
  Send-Your-Data key + domain so OTel spans land in ops's tenant, not
  the trainee's team tenant.
* Every key fetch is audit-logged as keys.*.fetched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
